definitions.text: A gentle bend, such as in a road.
examples: You should slow down when approaching a curve.

definitions.text: A simple figure containing no straight portions and no angles; a curved line.
examples: She scribbled a curve on the paper.

definitions.text: A grading system based on the scale of performance of a group used to normalize a right-skewed grade distribution (with more lower scores) into a bell curve, so that more can receive higher grades, regardless of their actual knowledge of the subject.
examples: The teacher was nice and graded the test on a curve.

definitions.text: To cause to swerve from a straight course.
examples: to curve a ball in pitching it

definitions.text: To bend or turn gradually from a given direction.
examples: the road curves to the right
